skill.name: sast-configuration
skill.description: Configure Static Application Security Testing (SAST) tools for automated vulnerability detection in application code. Use when setting up security scanning, implementing DevSecOps practices, or automating code vulnerability detection.
skill.summary: Set up SAST tools, rules, and CI gates for application security scanning.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell commands, a hardcoded URL, relative links, and heuristic combinations in SKILL.md. Review found these are Markdown examples for SAST setup, CI configuration, and related documentation, not executable automation or credential access. The skill is publishable with low risk because users may copy commands that install tools, run containers, or scan repositories.

low_findings.title: Documentation Includes Copyable Shell Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings at lines 73, 84, 101, 109, 112, 119, 151, 153, 156, 163, and 166 are false positives for code execution. They are fenced Markdown examples for installing and running SAST tools, but users should still review commands before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears inside Markdown code fences and is not invoked by the skill itself. The examples match the stated SAST configuration purpose.

low_findings.title: Documentation References External Repository URL
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL at line 114 is a Semgrep pre-commit repository reference. This is expected configuration content and does not send data to an unauthorized endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is used as a documented pre-commit source for a known SAST tool. No evidence found of credential transmission or hidden network behavior.

low_findings.title: Relative Links Mistaken For Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings at lines 193, 194, and 195 are relative Markdown links to related skills. They do not read or write files and do not create a traversal exploit.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sequences are static documentation links, not parameters to filesystem APIs. No file access code exists in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Keyword And Heuristic Findings Are Benign
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, network reconnaissance, and combined obfuscation findings are false positives from security terminology. The file discusses SAST, SAML, network troubleshooting, and scanning practices without malicious instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review shows the suspicious terms are part of security tooling documentation. No evidence found of credential access, obfuscation, or malicious reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Run Commands In Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides commands for package installation, Docker execution, GitHub tooling, and Semgrep scans. These are not automatically executed, but users should run them only in approved repositories and CI environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are legitimate for the skill purpose, but they can affect local environments or produce scan artifacts. Human review is appropriate before copying them into production workflows.

content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable security scans that fit their languages and CI pipelines. This skill guides SAST setup, rule tuning, quality gates, and remediation workflows.
actual_capabilities: Helps choose SAST tools based on language support and integration needs.
Provides setup guidance for Semgrep, SonarQube, and CodeQL.
Explains CI pipeline integration and quality gate thresholds.
Guides custom rule creation and false positive tuning.
Covers SARIF processing, compliance scans, and remediation tracking.
limitations: Does not replace manual security review or penetration testing.
Requires access to source code and CI configuration.
Example referenced assets are not included in this package.
Tool behavior depends on installed versions and project languages.

use_cases.title: Add SAST To A New Service
use_cases.description: Create an initial scanning plan, select tools, and add CI checks for a new repository.

use_cases.title: Tune Security Gates
use_cases.description: Set severity thresholds, reduce false positives, and define when builds should fail.

use_cases.title: Prepare Compliance Evidence
use_cases.description: Run focused scans, export results, and track remediation for audit requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Choose A SAST Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a SAST tool for a repository using [languages]. My CI system is [CI system], and my main requirement is [requirement].

prompt_templates.title: Create A CI Scan Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a SAST pipeline for [repository type]. Include scan timing, severity gates, artifact handling, and developer feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Tune False Positives
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these SAST findings and propose a tuning plan. Separate true risks, false positives, suppressions, and rule changes.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Multi-Tool Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SAST rollout plan using Semgrep, SonarQube, and CodeQL across [number] teams. Include governance, metrics, and remediation workflow.

output_examples.input: Set up SAST for a Python API in GitHub Actions.
output_examples.output: A tool recommendation, baseline scan approach, CI gate policy, artifact plan, and developer remediation workflow.

output_examples.input: Our Semgrep scan has many false positives.
output_examples.output: A triage plan that groups findings, documents suppressions, adjusts rule scope, and schedules recurring review.

output_examples.input: Prepare SAST evidence for PCI-DSS review.
output_examples.output: A compliance-focused scan checklist with severity handling, result retention, owner assignment, and remediation verification.

best_practices: Start with non-blocking baseline scans before enforcing build failures.
Document every suppression with owner, reason, and review date.
Keep scan artifacts and logs free of secrets and sensitive source data.
anti_patterns: Do not enable strict gates before teams understand the baseline.
Do not send private repositories to third-party scanners without approval.
Do not treat SAST findings as complete proof of application security.

faq.question: Which SAST tools does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Semgrep, SonarQube, and CodeQL with setup, tuning, and CI integration guidance.

faq.question: Can this skill write custom rules?
faq.answer: It can guide custom rule design and examples, especially for Semgrep and CodeQL workflows.

faq.question: Does this replace DAST or penetration testing?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on source code analysis and should complement runtime testing and manual review.

faq.question: Can it help reduce false positives?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains triage, path filters, suppressions, and rule tuning practices.

faq.question: Is it suitable for compliance programs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help plan evidence collection, severity handling, and remediation tracking.

faq.question: Does the skill execute scans automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and example commands that users must review before running.
